natural_language_query: Who are the top 10 customers by total amount spent?
sql_query: SELECT u.first_name, u.last_name, u.email, ROUND(SUM(o.total_amount), 2) AS total_spent FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id WHERE o.status = 'Delivered' GROUP BY u.user_id, u.first_name, u.last_name, u.email ORDER BY total_spent DESC LIMIT 10;

natural_language_query: What is the monthly revenue for the year 2024?
sql_query: SELECT TO_CHAR(DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date), 'YYYY-MM') AS month, ROUND(SUM(total_amount), 2) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM order_date) = 2024 AND status = 'Delivered' GROUP BY DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) ORDER BY month;

natural_language_query: What are the best-selling products by total quantity sold in each category?
sql_query: WITH ranked AS (SELECT c.category_name, p.product_name, SUM(oi.quantity) AS total_sold, ROW_NUMBER() OVER (PARTITION BY c.category_id ORDER BY SUM(oi.quantity) DESC) AS rank FROM products p JOIN subcategories sc ON p.subcategory_id = sc.subcategory_id JOIN categories c ON sc.category_id = c.category_id JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.status != 'Cancelled' GROUP BY c.category_id, c.category_name, p.product_id, p.product_name) SELECT category_name, product_name, total_sold, rank FROM ranked WHERE rank <= 5 ORDER BY category_name, rank;

natural_language_query: Show me the month-over-month revenue growth percentage
sql_query: WITH monthly AS (SELECT DATE_TRUNC('month', order_date) AS month, SUM(total_amount) AS revenue FROM orders WHERE status = 'Delivered' GROUP BY 1) SELECT TO_CHAR(month, 'YYYY-MM') AS month, ROUND(revenue, 2) AS revenue, ROUND(LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month), 2) AS prev_revenue, ROUND(((revenue - LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month)) / NULLIF(LAG(revenue) OVER (ORDER BY month), 0)) * 100, 2) AS growth_pct FROM monthly ORDER BY month;

natural_language_query: What is the average order value grouped by the year users signed up?
sql_query: SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM u.signup_date) AS signup_year, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS total_orders, ROUND(AVG(o.total_amount), 2) AS avg_order_value FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id WHERE o.status != 'Pending' GROUP BY EXTRACT(YEAR FROM u.signup_date) ORDER BY signup_year DESC;

natural_language_query: Find active users who have never placed an order
sql_query: SELECT u.user_id, u.first_name, u.last_name, u.email, u.signup_date FROM users u LEFT JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id WHERE u.is_active = TRUE AND o.order_id IS NULL ORDER BY u.signup_date DESC;

natural_language_query: What are the top 5 suppliers by total revenue from their products?
sql_query: SELECT s.company_name, ROUND(SUM(oi.quantity * oi.unit_price), 2) AS total_revenue FROM suppliers s JOIN products p ON s.supplier_id = p.supplier_id JOIN order_items oi ON p.product_id = oi.product_id JOIN orders o ON oi.order_id = o.order_id WHERE o.status = 'Delivered' GROUP BY s.supplier_id, s.company_name ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 5;

natural_language_query: How many orders are there per status type?
sql_query: SELECT status, COUNT(*) AS order_count, ROUND(100.0 * COUNT(*) / SUM(COUNT(*)) OVER (), 2) AS percentage FROM orders GROUP BY status ORDER BY order_count DESC;

natural_language_query: What is the average product rating for each category?
sql_query: SELECT c.category_name, ROUND(AVG(pr.rating), 2) AS avg_rating, COUNT(pr.review_id) AS total_reviews FROM categories c JOIN subcategories sc ON c.category_id = sc.category_id JOIN products p ON sc.subcategory_id = p.subcategory_id JOIN product_reviews pr ON p.product_id = pr.product_id GROUP BY c.category_id, c.category_name ORDER BY avg_rating DESC;

natural_language_query: Show me total revenue and order count for each country
sql_query: SELECT u.country, COUNT(DISTINCT o.order_id) AS total_orders, ROUND(SUM(o.total_amount), 2) AS total_revenue FROM users u JOIN orders o ON u.user_id = o.user_id WHERE o.status = 'Delivered' GROUP BY u.country ORDER BY total_revenue DESC LIMIT 20;

natural_language_query: Which departments have the highest average employee salary?
sql_query: SELECT d.department_name, COUNT(e.employee_id) AS employee_count, ROUND(AVG(e.salary), 2) AS avg_salary, ROUND(MAX(e.salary), 2) AS max_salary FROM departments d JOIN employees e ON d.department_id = e.department_id GROUP BY d.department_id, d.department_name ORDER BY avg_salary DESC;

natural_language_query: What products have low stock? Show products with less than 20 units
sql_query: SELECT p.product_name, p.stock_quantity, p.price, s.company_name AS supplier FROM products p JOIN suppliers s ON p.supplier_id = s.supplier_id WHERE p.stock_quantity < 20 ORDER BY p.stock_quantity ASC LIMIT 50;
